name: ZSUN Base Builder 2
description:
Join one of ZSUN’s longest-running and most loved events – BaseBuilder 2. Designed to help you build or maintain aerobic endurance, this ride typically covers 100km (shorter for E pen) at a consistent Zone 2 pace, with friendly chat and helpful advice throughout.

Expect a welcoming atmosphere, steady pacing (apart from the ups and downs), and a large group of regular riders. Drafting is essential, so stay close and work together!

🗓️
Saturday | ~1.8–3.2 w/kg | 90–120 mins

💬
Regular chat & tips | Join Discord for voice comms

🎁
Complete the ride to earn your ZSUN kit

🏁
Pens A–E available | All riders welcome

⚠️
Fence may be used to keep the group together

Whether you're building fitness or enjoying the social side, this ride has it all.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN Base Builder 2 (A)
eventSubgroups.description: The ride targets people who are looking to maintain an average pace of 3.3 w/kg. On short climbs we will usually stand and push to about 3.7 w/kg and on the downhills we relax and recover a bit at 3.0 w/kg. We try to stay together allowing us to move faster as a large pack.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN Base Builder 2 (B)
eventSubgroups.description: Lead by me Scott McVeigh aka Nanny! - The ride targets people who are looking to maintain an average pace of 2.7 w/kg. On short climbs we will usually stand and push to about 3.5 w/kg and on the downhills we relax and recover a bit at 2.5 w/kg. We try to stay together allowing us to move faster as a large pack.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN Base Builder 2 (C)
eventSubgroups.description: The ride targets people who are looking to maintain an average pace of 2.3 w/kg. On short climbs we will usually stand and push to about 3.0 w/kg and on the downhills we relax and recover a bit at 2.0 w/kg. We try to stay together allowing us to move faster as a large pack.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN Base Builder 2 (D)
eventSubgroups.description: The ride targets people who are looking to maintain an average pace of 2.0 w/kg. On short climbs we will usually stand and push to about 2.5 w/kg and on the downhills we relax and recover a bit at 1.7 w/kg.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN Base Builder 2 (E)
eventSubgroups.description: The ride targets people who are looking to maintain an average pace of 1.6 w/kg. To 1.8 w/kg. On short climbs we will usually stand and push to about 2.3 w/kg and on the downhills we relax and recover a bit at 1.5 w/kg. The Sweeper in this Pen will hold 1.4 w/kg. This Pen may be a shorter distance than the rest each week.

name: 111 CIEONTONCE v-race
description: Bienvenidos al 111 - Strongman racing series, una carrera virtual que tiene como fin mantener alta la motivación, entrenar de forma entretenida, y compartir con personas de Colombia y otros países. Tendremos rifas, premios y sorpresas para los participantes que completen el evento.

name: Catrina's Endurance Ride
description:
I would like to invite ALL women to join this Ride & Social exchange! Our goals during the ride are as follows:

Socialize with other women as well as complete a goal of riding a 100 miles on Zwift or just doing a long endurance training ride together with other women.  

This is NOT a NO DROP ride.  With that said we do try in my rides to keep the pace steady and drop back if we know early enough to bring back dropped riders.  We encourage all women to join to get stronger together. 

Ultimately my goal is to enhance your endurance training while having FUN!

Catrina Weiss (Group Leader)

We look forward to meeting & riding with you ladies as always!

name: Alpe 75 & 85 min Pace & Chase 50/45/40.
description:
As Zwift is a great community Torturefest Cult & Team GIANT are
collaborating to help you get stronger.

For the Torturefest rides we have added a Twist, stronger riders will be
chasing you down. Start times are set so they can catch you in the final
segments.

Pen E is the advertised paced ride with beacons
Pen D is a 85 min pace (beacons not guaranteed)
Pen C is the 50 minute pace chase (beacons not guaranteed)
Pen B is the 45 minute pace chase (beacons not guaranteed)
Pen A is the sub 40 minute pace (beacons not guaranteed)

Alpe Paced Rides

Wanna build your Alpe pace but want a wheel to follow?
Join our rides where our beacons will pace you at 4 different advertised
paces across 4 rolling weekends, 2 paces per weekend at 8am CT and 9.30 am CT.

Hold pace until ya crack, then come back again and get to extend the
cracking point.

We deliver Personal Records / Bests.

The goal is to build up to the magic sub 60 mins for Alpe, faster and
slower riders are always welcome.

This is a rolling 4-week schedule

Weekend #1
8.00 am CT 60 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 75 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #2
8.00 am CT 65 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 70 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #3
8.00 am CT 70 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 65 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #4
8.00 am CT 75 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 60 min pace - Team GIANT

Pro tips

1) Look at your best Alpe time on Strava, there will be a segment for
the entire
climb with average watts for the entire climb. Beating that average
watts number
by 5 watts each segment is your goal for each segment.

2) Use all the tools in the toolbox:
• Cardiovascular = Seated higher rpm
• Legs = Seated low rpm – Great for the knees!!!!
• Legs = Standing low rpm – You will crack

3) We go warm for the first 25%, a steady pace for the until 75% and push at
the end, start out too hot and you’ll suffer at the top.

name: AP Racing | Saturday Ride Along
description:
Ever wanted to know what it is like to train like a Pro? Well now you can! These workouts are designed for Andy Potts and his racing team! These are set for all skill levels and are ""no drop"" so as long as you are pedaling, you are with the group! We can't promise you will get to a podium but Andy does have over 30 Half Distance Tri's so it couldn't hurt!

Just a relaxin' stroll through Zwift land! Grab a bottle and let's #getsome!

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Slowpoke Soiree p/b Ascenders
description:
Our Team would like to cordially invite you to join our nice weekend club ride that will cruise at a slower speed of 15mph/24kph range. This is designed to be an enjoyable event for you to ride with like minded people.

While this will be an all out effort for some, we have kind and helpful riders who are there to lend a wheel whenever needed. For others, it may be just right or a recovery pace. Whatever your reason may be for joining us, we look forward to you give us a try.

# Join Ascenders Team on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
# Ascenders Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Redrock Loop
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off at Redrock Loop on the brand-new Gravel Mountain map. A fast, punchy gravel test to set the tone. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Redrock Loop Reverse
Date: April 27 – May 3

Back for more on the brand new Gravel Mountain map. One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Run In The Park
description:
Come and join the original Zwift group run! 

This event is a 5km mass start event and runs at multiple times every Saturday.  

It is open to runners of all abilities so whether you are a recreational runner or an elite athlete you are welcome on the start line.  

Get to the start gates early to chat with the other runners, you may find someone of a similar ability to group up with for the run.  

There is no time limit for completing the event so run at your own pace and enjoy the course.

name: Triathletes on Zwift Saturday Endurance Workout
description:
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

This workout is part of a 30-week progressive series that builds from tempo to sub threshold focused workout efforts.  The aim of this series is to build riders long ride aerobic abilities, specifically to enable them to effectively get off the bike prepared to run.  Total ride time over the 24 build series weeks will extend from 1hr :15 min up to 3 hours.  As workouts get longer we will incorporate higher effort/shorter duration intervals (super threshold work) into the program after the main set.  These workouts are perfect for long course focused triathletes along with anyone interested in developing their abilities to ride for longer periods of time.  Each of the 24-week workouts builds off the preceding workout in terms of duration of main set intervals and total ride time.

WORKOUT DESIGNER

AJ Baucco is a well established professional triathlete from Cleveland, Ohio. He founded AJ Baucco Coaching, which has coached over 150 triathletes in the last 5 years. He also manages 3 different age group triathlon teams; The Baucco Squad, TRS Racing, and Team INFINIT Performance. Over the years, AJ Baucco has become a premier triathlon coach. He has helped athletes achieve personal bests, stand on podiums, and even race world championship events. On the race course, AJ Baucco has achieved top results at every distance from super sprint to Ironman. Most recently, he was on the winning team for 6 different Major League Triathlon events, and 2 back to back series championships.

Contact – 
Websites – www.baucco.com, www.ajbcoaching.com, www.bauccosquad.com
Twitter - @irunshirtless
Instagram - @ajbaucco

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins.  Everyone stays together regardless of power output.   That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other.  Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.

HELPFUL HINT

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level.   

For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

eventSubgroups.name: Triathletes on Zwift Saturday Endurance Workout (E)

name: ZLDR Weekly Long Run
description:
Welcome to the ZLDR Weekly Long Run. ZLDR is a group of diverse runners, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners who will make sure that you are never left behind.

This distance of this run changes depending on the week. For most Saturdays, the event distance will be 13.1 miles/21.1 km. On the first Saturday of every month our run will go to 26.2 miles / 42.2 km -- that’s a full marathon. We keep this run on routes that are faily short loops so that everyone can stay within chat distance of each other no matter what speed you are running or walking.

Of course, as with all of our runs, you are not required to go the entire distance listed. Feel free to run as long as you would like, but as a distance running group we like to offer our members a chance every month to put their legs to the test and see how far they can go.

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook group:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Instagram:
https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Strava:
https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020

https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn

name: Group Run 5k
description:
DESCRIPTION

Join us for a 5k group run.   It's run-at-your-own-pace and all are welcome! 

Pick the closest pace group for you, but all groups will start together and you are free to fall back and run ahead. The pace groups designate average pace for the duration of the run. Most groups will start slower and finish faster than the indicated pace. Feel free to mix in some intervals and race your friends.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Times Square Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Team CLS Base 'n' Banter
description:
Base ’n’ Banter is a controlled weekend endurance ride - with a strong community and social focus! 

Across every weekend in 2026, we will continue to offer a mix of rides that cover flat, rolling and hilly terrain with the aim of getting in a solid endurance ride, mostly around 2hrs.

This is a chance to enjoy a friendly experienced group club ride with regulars who have no Big Egos. While you build your fitness, there is always plenty of in-ride banter. 

What pace to expect?
We will ride the flats around 2.5 - 2.7 w/kg with a focus on keeping everyone together!

The leader will climb at around 3.0 - 3.2 w/kg depending on the length and if needed we will always try and regroup after.

While the leader will do everything to keep everyone together, we can't slow repeatedly so some splits are inevitable.

Our SWEEPERS on this ride, known as our ‘ORANGE ARMY’ (OA), are members of TEAM CLS who are dedicated to helping others. If you have had a drop out or accidentally hit a wrong key, type ‘HELP’ and they will come back and offer you a wheel back to the group.

Note: While we always try to ensure this ride is led by one of our beacons there may be some weeks where this is not possible. The ride always has regulars that will ride to the advertised pace!


WEEKLY PROGRAM
Mon  18:50 UK - Team CLS Racing - Pace ‘n’ Race: Recon and Activation 
Tues 18:50 UK - This ‘n’ That - Endurance Training
Wed  18:50 UK - Ride ‘n’ Explore Club Rideout
Thur 18:50 UK - High ‘n’ Low - Interval Training
Sat  08:50 and 14:45 UK - Base ‘n’ Banter Endurance ride
Sun  18:50 UK - Chill 'n' Spin - Weekend Social



TEAM CLS LINKS
Check out www.teamclscc.co.uk ...find out about our club, current events and leaders. 

Facebook Club Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclscc
Facebook Racing Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclsracing
Zwift Power Team - https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=1426



TEAM | CLS - Training | Racing | Community

name: DIRT Dadurday Chase Race
description:
Welcome to the DIRT Dadurday Chase Race.

Race category starts will be staggered with the E category starting first, and each D, C, B, and A category starting later.

The riders will be challenged to work together using the draft, and group tactics in order to catch the category ahead of them while also holding off the categories that started after. 

Races will have 4 power-ups enabled with equal odds (25% each): 
draft, feather, burrito, & ghost! 
Compete with the best, and see how you stack up!

You must be opted-in on Zwift.com & ZwiftPower.com to be counted for race results.
We highly encourage A racers to follow Zwift eRacing Ruleset.
Dual recording of trainer & power meter, and recent weigh-in on ZwifTransparency 
recommended--we reserve the right to ask for this information to verify outstanding 
performances.

Categories are based on Zwift Racing Score:
A: 800 - 1000
B: 600 - 800
C: 400 - 600
D: 200 - 400
E: 0 - 200

name: 3R Endurance Steady Ride
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

name: Ride On with Team Type 1 Social Ride
description:
The ride duration is 60 minutes and the ride leader will strictly adhere to a steady 1.5-2.0w/kg pace.

ZWIFT Team Type 1 is affiliated with Team Type 1 Foundation and comprised of riders with Type 1 Diabetes or who have a close connection to someone with the condition. If Type 1 Diabetes touches your life please consider joining our ZWIFT team at https://www.facebook.com/groups/1365148016828687/

We are focused on building a community to support one another and to raise awareness within the greater ZWIFT community by riding and/or racing together. We are excited to have you join us!! Ride on.

TAKE ACTION/DONATIONS
Fulfilling the TT1 mission is possible only through your help to save children within developing countries. Even now, in the 21st century, many still suffer and die from diabetes, yet all that is needed are the tools and medication to manage the disease. Your donation also will provide continuing studies for athletes with diabetes, who want to take care of their own health while educating others about how best to do the same.
http://teamtype1.org/take-action-donations/

#teamtype1

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit || Advanced
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!

Whether you like to race or get fitter, this ride is for you. We like to focus on 2hrs of zone 2 but as this ride is banded, you can ride as you wish and stay with the group. It’s the most social of all the ride types!

We would love for you to join our Zwift club, Herts County Triathletes found here: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/03798a9d-0f51-4e26-ac44-1727553f12b1/home

name: CCBoucherville-V2000 FUN Races
description:
Club Cycliste Boucherville (CCB) in Quebec (Canada) is a non-profit organization to promote cycling as a social and healthy lifestyle. We help kids to acquire the knowledge and skills that will allow to develop themselves as cyclists. Although we prioritize the pleasure at training, we also make sure to support those who wish to participate in races, regardless of the objectives. Young riders can join us from the age of 5... as long as they want to have fun on two wheels. The first year, we can even provide them with a bike for free - thanks to Vélo-2000 Garneau Concept (https://velo2000.qc.ca/)).

This weekly race is meant to be an opportunity to ride and have fun with teammates and opponents during the cold season, and meet passionate cyclists from around the world.

Race your category (based on your best estimation of your FTP/kg).

- A: 4.0W/kg and OVER (Red Circle Icon)  --  3 laps
- B: 3.2W/kg to 3.99W/kg (Green Circle Icon)  --  3 laps
- C: 2.5W/kg to 3.19W/kg (Blue Circle Icon)  --  2 laps
- D: UNDER 2.5W/kg (Yellow Circle Icon)  --  1 lap

Be prepared for 1, 2 or 3 laps (around 10 km each). The course is relatively flat. Start strong and grab a good wheel.

All bikes allowed

PowerUp allowed

Heart Rate Monitor recommended

Unfair and unsporting behaviour prohibited

Fun and Lactate mandatory

Results will be available here and on zwiftpower.com. These races do not count towards any league standings.

Join us on our Discord Channel (https://discord.gg/DZDVFVhto) to discuss your race strategy during the warm-up… and chat during the race if you have enough energy. Use the GENERAL channel, or discuss more privately with your Bike Club teammates in your respective channel. Welcome to French- and English-speaking riders. Please use push-to-talk. 

Please visit our website https://clubcyclisteboucherville.com/ and our FaceBook page https://www.facebook.com/clubcyclisteboucherville/ to share pictures, comments and suggestions.

Ride Hard. Have Fun.

#sympatique #CCB #velo2000 #FunRace

name: EVR Winter Series
description:
Welcome to the EVR Winter Series. During these novel times where
outdoor, in-person racing is not possible, we wanted to provide a continued
sense of community through organizing a weekly race series to allow
everyone the chance to race together, albeit indoors separately. We'll keep
track of your efforts as you keep training and racing indoors - you just
keep coming back for more!

Give us a shout if this is your first time here and don't know what to do.
For the experienced, we suggest the typical Zwift recommendations for power:

CATEGORIES

A: 4.0-6.0 w/kg
B: 3.2-3.9 w/kg
C: 2.5-3.1 w/kg
D: 1.0- 2.49 w/kg
E: Women Only (all w/kg)

eventSubgroups.name: EVR Winter Series (A)
eventSubgroups.description: #doubledraft

name: Applewood Cycling Saturday Chase Race
description:
If you enjoy the Applewood Manor Cycling weekly group ride, and you are ready to take advantage of your Douce course riding skills and put them to the race test then join Applewood Manor Cycling for our Saturday epic race.  

Double the distance and double the challenge of our popular group ride.   Take on 4 laps of the Douce course covering approximately 100km in just over 2 hours.    Similar to our weekly group ride, everyone will start together and fly around the course with a little help from #doubledraft.    

This is one of the fastest long distance races on Zwift with average speeds close to 48kph for the A group.   

Please join our Applewood Cycling club on Strava and follow us  Instagram @applewood_manor.  Visit our Facebook page at https://www.facebook.com/theapplewoodmanor for information about our B&B and our cycling program and to leave comments and suggestions.  Website: www.applewoodmanor.com

name: Team DRAFT Saturday Race
description:
Welcome to the Saturday Crit Race brought to you by Team DRAFT!
We encourage that all participants head over to zwiftpower.com and register. Riders who have not completed the Zwiftpower registration will not be included in the official results. We also ask that your Zwift activities be saved publicly, and that you open and maintain a Strava account to allow for organizer analysis.
RULES:
- No zPower riders.
- Heart rate monitors mandatory for all categories
- 5min and 20min CEVAZ limits apply. Riding in the wrong category will lead to disqualification.
RACE CATEGORIES:
A: 4.0 W/kg to 5.0 W/kg
B: 3.2 W/kg to 3.99 W/kg
C: 2.5 W/kg to 3.19 W/kg
D: Under 2.49 W/kg

name: Alpe 60 min Pace 😈🍍
description:
As Zwift is a great community Torturefest Cult & Team GIANT are collaborating
to help you get stronger.

Wanna build your Alpe pace but want a wheel to follow?
Join our rides where our beacons will pace you at 4 different advertised paces across 4 rolling weekends, 2 paces per weekend at 8am CT and 9.30am CT.

We do have unofficial slower and faster paces just ask in the pen and if we have available pacers we will pace a 2nd pace as well.

Hold pace until ya crack, then come back again and get to extend the cracking point. 
We deliver Personal Records / Bests.

The goal is to build up to the magic sub 60 mins for Alpe, faster and slower riders are always welcome.

This is a rolling 4-week schedule 

Weekend #1
8.00 am CT 60 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 75 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #2
8.00 am CT 65 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 70 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #3
8.00 am CT 70 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 65 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #4
8.00 am CT 75 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 60 min pace - Team GIANT


Pro tips

1) Look at your best Alpe time on Strava, there will be a segment for the
entire climb with average watts for the entire climb. Beating that average watt
number by 5 watts each segment is your goal for each segment.

2) Use all the tools in the toolbox:

• Cardiovascular = Seated higher rpm

• Legs = Seated low rpm – Great for the knees!!!!

• Legs = Standing low rpm – You will crack

3) We go warm for the first 25%, a steady pace for the until 75% and push
at the end, start out to hot you’ll suffer at the top.

name: GXY SUMMITS & SWITCHBACKS [2.3 ~ 3.0 wkg] Cat C
description:
Welcome to GALAXY and our Summits and Switchbacks group ride . This weekly ride will target climbing , undulating and occasionally flat and badge hunt courses too.
Jump on, settle in and respect the leader’s pace and instructions, the time or distance will fly by!
GXY are blessed with super sweepers, if you need a wheel at any point, shout early and our sweepers will be on hand to support.
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed. We are looking forward to riding with you.
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally via our Facebook link or by the email below.

name: EVO CC Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO race series. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: PACK🐺 SUB2 + KOM After Party
description:
This PACK SUB2 event is aimed at Zwifters looking for a friendly group ride experience with a pace that matches the description of less than 2w/kg. It’s a great introduction to group riding with full pace instructions via text and voice chat using discord. You can sign up early via the web or mobile app and late join is enabled.

After the event finishes, there is an optional fun After Party KOM challenge up the final climb. Help pace others or try to beat your HoloReplay.

All riders will appear in the same kit and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please respect the pace set by the leader, this is a group ride not a race. #strongertogether

Finally, if you are just starting your zwift experience, please join our facebook group and PACK Club for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

PACK Club:
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/Pack/join

Facebook Group #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

PACK Events:
https://zwift.com/events/tag/pack

Discord Voice Chat:
https://discord.gg/cce7ers

Zwiftpower Team:
https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=2928

name: Bicycle Way of Life Saturday Ride
description:
Bicycle Way of Life Saturday Morning 100K

This group ride is not a race, rather it is a ride intended as a D level opportunity for a metric century.  Our pace can best be described as endurance pace, typically on a relatively flat course.  We always plan to provide lead and sweep support, but there will be times where that will not be the case.  You are encouraged to pace with the ride leader/yellow beacon, but if they are not able to make the ride, or if you find the pace too quick, drop off, group up, work together and find your way to 100K.  Teamwork makes this ride work.
 
Want to know more about us? Bicycle Way of Life (bicycleway.com) is a local bike store chain in Eugene, Oregon. We encourage new and experienced riders to support each other indoors and out, fixing trails or just their bikes.
Interact and find more info. by joining our FB group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/bwolzwift
Look us up on Strava https://www.strava.com/clubs/bicycleway-com, Instagram https://www.instagram.com/bicyclewayoflife/, and Facebook https://www.facebook.com/bicycleway/

name: Tiny Race (1 of 4) by Zwift Insider
description:
IMPORTANT: read these notes to perform your best and avoid disqualification!

Tiny Races are mean to be raced as set of 4 back-to-back events that finish within one hour. Each race is on a different route that takes ~7-12 minutes to complete, and races are scheduled 15 minutes apart.

3 RULES:
- No skipping then returning. You will receive a DQ if you skip race 1, 2, or 3 but finish a later race in the set.
- You must have an active ZwiftPower account to compete for the overall podium.
- Smart trainer or power meter is required.

THIS WEEK'S ROUTES:
Race 1: Flat Route (5.23km, ends at bridge after Fishing Village) - Powerup: Steamroller
Race 2: Beach Island Loop (6.166km, ends at JWB Sprint) - Powerup: Draft Boost
Race 3: Seaside Sprint (5.676km, ends at Volcano Circuit arch) - Powerup: Aero Boost
Race 4: Road to Sky (5.5km, ends well before first Alpe hairpin) - Powerup: Feather

PRO TIPS: 
You will receive a single powerup at the start of each race, as listed above. Be sure to visit https://zwiftinsider.com/tiny/ for details on this week's routes, including custom finish line locations.

RESULTS:
You earn points in each race based on your finishing position. The overall category winners each week (available on ZwiftPower) are the riders with the most points across all 4 races. ZwiftPower result links:
- Zone 1 (9am UTC): https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=1172
- Zone 2 (3pm UTC): https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=1173
- Zone 3 (9pm UTC): https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=1174

name: Saris Fresh Legs
description:
Welcome to the Saris Fresh Legs Ride! 

This group ride is an introduction to indoor riding & training for those new to the virtual world. The Saris mission is to break down barriers so that everyone has access to fall in love with the bike. These rides are an unintimidating gateway into the world of virtual riding. 

We'll keep a social pace between 0.5 and 1.5 w/kg and each ride will be led by a Saris employee or ambassador who will keep the rides fun & interactive. If you're new to riding indoors and have questions feel free to use the Zwift Companion App to ask them during the ride.

This Saris kit will be unlocked for everyone who finishes the ride!

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: NoPinz R3R - 60km Race
description:
Welcome to this NoPinz R3R Race. The rules are simple....
Please use ZwiftPower's Minimum Category on your profile for race entry.
Cats A, and B will require a power source (power meter or smart trainer) and heart rate monitor.
Cat C will require power source (power meter or smart trainer) and heart rate monitor for podium spots.
Podium finishers in Cat A are expected to dual (power) record and to make analysis publicly available on ZwiftPower; they must also be using a direct drive trainer or power meter.
**Elite level performances are also expected to be supported by dual (power) recordings from reputable power sources or easily verifiable outside data. This applies to those with a history of such performances.
** 5.2 w/kg (20 mins); 6 w/kg (5 mins); 9.8 w/kg (1 min)
Zwift Activities must be saved publicly.
Please also maintain an open and active Strava profile to facilitate organiser inquiry.
Please see http://www.zwiftpower.com for finalised race results. The race is brought to you by NoPinz please visit https://nopinz.com/product-tag/subzero/ All subzero kit can be custom made in any design.

name: 🚴‍♂️ SWX Saturday Easy Free Ride ☀️
description:
Ease into the weekend with the SWX Saturday Social — a relaxed 60-minute group free ride built around connection, conversation, and steady miles.
We roll steady at 2.2–2.7 W/kg on rotating scenic routes with plenty of drafting, mellow climbs, and space to spin your legs. No pressure, no drops — just good company and smooth riding with your SWX crew.

• All levels welcome
• Stay-together mode enabled
• Late join allowed

Connect and learn more about who we are:
📸 Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/shark_worx_swx
🌐 Website: https://bit.ly/Visit_Coach_Cindy

name: Tiny Race (3 of 4) by Zwift Insider
description:
IMPORTANT: read these notes to perform your best and avoid disqualification!

Tiny Races are mean to be raced as set of 4 back-to-back events that finish within one hour. Each race is on a different route that takes ~7-12 minutes to complete, and races are scheduled 15 minutes apart.

3 RULES:
- No skipping then returning. You will receive a DQ if you skip race 1, 2, or 3 but finish a later race in the set.
- You must have an active ZwiftPower account to compete for the overall podium.
- Smart trainer or power meter is required.

THIS WEEK'S ROUTES:
Race 1: Flat Route (5.23km, ends at bridge after Fishing Village) - Powerup: Steamroller
Race 2: Beach Island Loop (6.166km, ends at JWB Sprint) - Powerup: Draft Boost
Race 3: Seaside Sprint (5.676km, ends at Volcano Circuit arch) - Powerup: Aero Boost
Race 4: Road to Sky (5.5km, ends well before first Alpe hairpin) - Powerup: Feather

PRO TIPS: 
You will receive a single powerup at the start of each race, as listed above. Be sure to visit https://zwiftinsider.com/tiny/ for details on this week's routes, including custom finish line locations.

RESULTS:
You earn points in each race based on your finishing position. The overall category winners each week (available on ZwiftPower) are the riders with the most points across all 4 races. ZwiftPower result links:
- Zone 1 (9am UTC): https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=1172
- Zone 2 (3pm UTC): https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=1173
- Zone 3 (9pm UTC): https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=1174

name: T-VGN 🌱 Sprout & Sprint
description: Welcome to Team Vegan’s Sunday Sprout & Sprint race. This is a chance to test yourself with race events each week. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

name: Fearless Womens 5K Race
description:
What's your best 5K time? Challenge yourself against other Zwift women runners and find out what you are made of!

One run can change your day, many runs can change your life.

Join to challenge yourself and for the race fun!!!

Hosted by Team Fearless. To hear about our other rides and runs or learn more about our women's team, head to our Facebook Fans page at https://www.facebook.com/groups/TeamFearlessZR/

name: Velos Race Of Truth
description:
This energy-sapping event pits riders against each other in a race they cycle alone. The individual time trial is referred to as the 'race of truth' because it reveals your own strength without the aid of draft from fellow riders. With breakneck speed valued above everything else, prepare to be amazed by this fast-paced spectacle.

Race Rules and Information
Race the same course each month to easily establish your improvement each week.
Riders will enter the category allowed by Zwift category Enforcement.
TT Bikes only
Only Riders in your category will be visible and without any draft. Z power and riders without heart rate monitors will be removed from the results.
Completing the race will unlock the Team Velos kit.

Join our community at www.teamvelos.co.uk

description:
¿Buscas un evento pausado para terminar la semana? ¿Recuperar fuerzas tras una sesión intensa? ¡Bienvenid@ a nuestro evento más relajado! Ritmo suave y sin tirones en torno a 1.5 wkg, rutas sin complicaciones y la mejor compañía te esperan en nuestras tardes de sábado.

ACTIVE FENCE

name: US Military Endurance Sports Fun Run
description: Welcome to the US Military Endurance Sports weekly “Fun Run”. This is a run at your own pace event that will explore different Zwift worlds weekly at varying distances and/or times. The leader typically starts at a 9:15 pace with added increments at a faster pace along the way. Our goal is to provide a platform for all to enjoy while increasing awareness about the USMES organization which is an endurance-based team of Activeduty military, veterans including friends and family. Activity Duty members are encouraged to place *USMES* in their Zwift name and to check out our Facebook Group at UZR2- USMES Zwift Ride and Run. #FitForDutyFitForLife

description:
⚡️🚴🏼‍💨 INOX Masters Trilogy  by Team INOX ⚡️🚴🏼‍💨

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Team Italy Burn Calories Together!
description:
Team Italy – Burning Calories Together is a welcoming social group ride where we keep things fun and supportive. The lead yellow beacon sets a steady pace between 1.5–2.0 w/kg and keeps the group together using the fence. Our sweeper also carries a yellow beacon to ensure all messages of help and encouragement are clearly visible to every rider. Together our ride leaders, supported by ITA friends, make sure no one is left behind.
It’s a relaxed atmosphere with plenty of friendly chat where riders look out for each other. Whether you’re building fitness, spinning the legs, or just enjoying good company, this ride is for everyone — so come share the fun and burn calories together!

Team Italy – Burning Calories Together è una pedalata di gruppo sociale e accogliente, pensata per divertirsi e sostenersi a vicenda. Il leader, con beacon giallo, mantiene un’andatura costante tra 1,5 e 2,0 w/kg e tiene il gruppo compatto utilizzando la fence. Anche lo sweeper avrà il Beacon giallo, così tutti i messaggi di aiuto e incoraggiamento saranno chiaramente visibili a ogni partecipante. Insieme, i nostri leader, supportati dagli amici ITA, faranno in modo che nessuno venga lasciato indietro.
L’atmosfera sarà rilassata, con una chat piacevole, e i ciclisti si prenderanno cura gli uni degli altri. Che tu stia migliorando la forma fisica, sciogliendo le gambe o semplicemente godendoti la buona compagnia, questa pedalata è per tutti — quindi vieni a condividere il divertimento e a bruciare calorie insieme!

eventSubgroups.description:
Categories' Leader will keep the target pace, choose if join them pace or go by yourself at your own pace, but Keeping the Leader pace assure draft and power savings.
You can see both the categories and try to beat your limits....
You'll find the discord's server into our Club, direct link, or introduce yourself and chat with us on Companion.
Please add the tag ITA, you'll earn the Team Italy Jersey if you finish the event.

name: RollCo: Rolling Tide - 12K
description:
🌊 ROLLING TIDE
Start small. Build big.
Welcome to Rolling Tide — RollCo’s season-long ritual race.

Every week, the distance grows by 1 km.
It begins as a short, explosive opener and steadily evolves into a full-distance endurance epic by year’s end.
This isn’t a one-off event — it’s a shared journey that rises with you.

🏁 Race Format
Rolling Tide is a categorised scratch race.
All riders start together, with categories keeping the racing fair and fast.
The only thing that changes… is the distance.

Week 1 → 1 km
Week 52 → 52 km

Early weeks reward raw power.
Later weeks demand patience, grit, and endurance.
The tide never stops rising.

Rolling Tide is about more than today’s finish line.
It’s about showing up — week after week.
Seeing the same names.
Measuring how far you’ve come as the distance stretches.

You’ll build rivalries.
You’ll track your progress.
You’ll remember the day it all began.

And by the end of the season, everyone who stayed will share the same truth:

“We rode the whole tide together.”

Why Join Rolling Collective?
We’re more than a club — we’re a global community of riders who support each other, grow together, and celebrate every ride, whether it’s a grind, a badge hunt, or a race. Join us on Discord, connect with fellow riders, and be part of something bigger.

Forward, Together

name: The HERD Rehab Ride
description:
This is a banded ride so the group can stay together and support riders new to Zwift, or recovering from sickness, or injuries. The ride leader, the Yellow Beacon, will ride at the advertised pace and, unless you stop pedaling, the group will stay together as if held together by a rubber band. Those wishing to ride faster/harder may certainly do so but THIS IS NOT A RACE! We ride together as a Herd. Clean "Dad Jokes" are encouraged!
Have fun.

eventSubgroups.name: The HERD Rehab Ride (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
New to Zwift, just a little out of shape or recovering from injury? This is the group ride for you! All are welcome to join The Herd on this supportive and social event.

Respect the leader's pace and ride with the yellow beacon. This is NOT A RACE! Stronger riders are encouraged to fall back and help sweep the riders struggling in the back.

Join us on Discord during the ride: https://discord.gg/Dr7ZtPV (Please use Push-To-Talk)
Check us out on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/zHerd/

#herd

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Redrock Loop
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off at Redrock Loop on the brand-new Gravel Mountain map. A fast, punchy gravel test to set the tone. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Redrock Loop Reverse
Date: April 27 – May 3

Back for more on the brand new Gravel Mountain map. One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Run In The Park
description:
Come and join the original Zwift group run! 

This event is a 5km mass start event and runs at multiple times every Saturday.  

It is open to runners of all abilities so whether you are a recreational runner or an elite athlete you are welcome on the start line.  

Get to the start gates early to chat with the other runners, you may find someone of a similar ability to group up with for the run.  

There is no time limit for completing the event so run at your own pace and enjoy the course.

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Neon After Party
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

Zwift TT Club Racing - Neon After Party (C)
Zwift TT Club Racing - Neon After Party (D)

name: OWL.BiKe Women's Grey Zone: Meet. Play. Ride.
description:
This is your weekly space to meet, play, and ride together while we spin in the sweet spot of the Grey Zone. Our rubber-banded rides keep things inclusive and welcoming, so no one gets dropped—just a smooth, social ride at an easy pace.

But this isn’t just a ride. Along the way, we bring in a playful, gamified quiz experience designed to spark conversation, curiosity, and connection. Topics range from light and fun to meaningful and empowering, with a focus on celebrating and uplifting women.

Come for the ride, stay for the vibe—this is where community, conversation, and movement meet.

name: GTR Epic Race
description:
This event will see you take on the Epic KOM in some form whether it be forwards or revere.

Do you have what it takes to take on this Iconic Zwift climb.

The route will change from week to week taking on some part of the Mountain.

Will you have the legs to take it on??

name: Vikings of Valhalla Run
description: Welcome to the Team Valhalla group run. Join us on 5 km running with Valhalla Vikings. Please join us weekly on Saturday afternoons for a 5km/h - 10km/h run. We are using yellow beacon that you can follow while running! The only rule we have is to HAVE FUN! For more information about our rides and runs please go to https://www.facebook.com/groups/503388660165279 or https://www.skaal.cc

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Times Square Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: EVO CC Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO race series. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: BC Bike Race Endurance Workout
description:
Since 2007, athletes from 65 different countries have been racing the Ultimate Singletrack Adventure in British Columbia, Canada and now, BC Bike Race is bringing you a new experience to Zwift.

The BC Bike Race Endurance Workout is a weekly ride designed to help build your endurance during the winter months in preparation for the summer to shred singletrack.

For more information please visit bcbikerace.com

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: RollCo: Next Steps
description:
This is where racing gets tight.

Next Steps races are designed for intermediate riders who are ready for serious competition but aren't chasing elite-level racing. With narrow ZRS bandings and carefully structured fields, the focus is simple: close, competitive racing  from start to finish.

No huge ability gaps. No blown-apart fields in the first five minutes. Just sustained, tactical, high-quality racing where positioning, pacing and decision-making matter.

If you've moved beyond beginner racing and want events where the feel is the same, this series is for you.

Hold the wheel. Stay sharp. Race smart.

Why Join Rolling Collective?
We’re more than a club — we’re a global community of riders who support each other, grow together, and celebrate every ride, whether it’s a grind, a badge hunt, or a race. Join us on Discord, connect with fellow riders, and be part of something bigger.

Forward, Together

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Redrock Loop
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off at Redrock Loop on the brand-new Gravel Mountain map. A fast, punchy gravel test to set the tone. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Redrock Loop Reverse
Date: April 27 – May 3

Back for more on the brand new Gravel Mountain map. One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: ZZRC Rollers Sub 2
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Rollers Sub 2. This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 1.7-1.9 on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints , but the average for the whole ride will be sub 2 .

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced .

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch .

This is where our awesome ZZRC sweeps and Ride Buddies will help you return to group using their draft. They will give you clear encouraging instructions to aid this process .

Our crew are a caring bunch who dedicated a lot of hours to this club , but want you to have the best ride experience on Zwift

You will be fully supported and encouraged throughout the ride by the Leader, Sweeps and Ride Buddies .

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you .

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group .

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON .


We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord channel for our sub 2
https://discord.gg/RJ3dhbjCEp


TOP TIPS

1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!

2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone

3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Neon After Party
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: T-VGN 🌱 Mashed Potatoes
description:
Join our Plant Powered Mashed Potato crew as they head out on another super friendly Saturday social ride.
Always supportive, always fun, and with a regular rotation of routes for you to enjoy you’re sure to enjoy this hour with the plant based community. The event leader will hold a constant pace throughout, but we regularly mix things up with sprint routes for those who want to bring the power, fun quizzes to test your general and plant based knowledge, and chases back to the group towards the end of the ride, if you’re feeling strong. All are welcome to join our rides. Come and spend an hour with the vegan community and experience what a friendly, welcoming group we are.

name: EVR Winter Series
description:
Welcome to the EVR Winter Series. During these novel times where
outdoor, in-person racing is not possible, we wanted to provide a continued
sense of community through organizing a weekly race series to allow
everyone the chance to race together, albeit indoors separately. We'll keep
track of your efforts as you keep training and racing indoors - you just
keep coming back for more!

Give us a shout if this is your first time here and don't know what to do.
For the experienced, we suggest the typical Zwift recommendations for power:

CATEGORIES

A: 4.0-6.0 w/kg
B: 3.2-3.9 w/kg
C: 2.5-3.1 w/kg
D: 1.0- 2.49 w/kg
E: Women Only (all w/kg)

name: HerD Beginner Races
description:
Where do you go to try racing or to grow a little better with your racing craft… Herd Beginner Races is set up to provide you with that opportunity by using Zwift Racing Scores (ZRS) with bandwidths that are set up so you’re competing with people of similarities and routes that enable you to challenge yourself and grow with racing. Our pens are set up as;

Pen A   ZRS  345-430

Pen B   ZRS 265-345

Pen C   ZRS 185-265

Pen D   ZRS 115-185

Pen E   ZRS 0-115

If you’re new to racing please take Zwift’s recommendation in blue.

Each week HBR races will have a different focus; racing on a flat route, rolling/undulating with a small climbs, and a route that is commonly used in many Zwift racing events. In addition, the last race of the month will be Tick-Tock so that you can monitor your performance.

name: Mountain Massif Group Run

Join Mountain Massif for some relaxed group runs. The goal is to simply complete the route and collect the badge.

More information:

name: ZSUN Saturday Social Ride
description:
The Saturday Social is a friendly, inclusive weekly ride for anyone looking to enjoy a steady-paced, community-focused Zwift experience.

This event welcomes all riders and encourages supportive group riding, friendly banter, and shared motivation. Led by experienced ride leaders and often supported by sweepers, it’s perfect for staying active and connected.

🗓️
Saturday | ~1.7–1.9 w/kg | ~60 mins

🌍
Open to all | No-drop ethos

💬
Great conversation & camaraderie

🎁
Complete the ride to earn your ZSUN kit

Just clip in and enjoy the ride – it’s what Saturdays are made for!

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Neon After Party
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: EVO CC Sprint Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO sprintrace series. Shorter more compact races for the rider looking for an effective high intensity race. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: Weekend Roll Call with Team Giant
description:
Whether you are looking for a recovery ride, or looking to build your endurance on the bike, at Team Giant we have you covered on this steady social ride that is the Weekend Roll Call. 
Bring your best support, banter and willingness to work with the group, to help everyone succeed and reach their potential.

eventSubgroups.name: Weekend Roll Call with Team Giant  (C)

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Times Square Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: EVR Winter Series
description:
Welcome to the EVR Winter Series. During these novel times where
outdoor, in-person racing is not possible, we wanted to provide a continued
sense of community through organizing a weekly race series to allow
everyone the chance to race together, albeit indoors separately. We'll keep
track of your efforts as you keep training and racing indoors - you just
keep coming back for more!

Give us a shout if this is your first time here and don't know what to do.
For the experienced, we suggest the typical Zwift recommendations for power:

CATEGORIES

A: 4.0-6.0 w/kg
B: 3.2-3.9 w/kg
C: 2.5-3.1 w/kg
D: 1.0- 2.49 w/kg
E: Women Only (all w/kg)

eventSubgroups.name: EVR Winter Series (A)
eventSubgroups.description: #doubledraft

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: No Spring Chickens - Herd Masters Racing
description:
No Spring Chickens - Herd Masters Racing
No Spring Chickens is back for another round of masters racing! This is your chance to compare
yourself to riders that are the same age. It doesn’t matter if you have passed the spring chicken
stage only recently or a little longer ago, this is a race for you.
No Spring Chickens is a mass start race, all age groups will have their own starting pen, but they will
all take off together. After the finish the results for men and women will be split on ZwiftPower. That
way we hope everyone will find someone to race with and have some fun.
Series schedule:
20-22 March: Wandering Flats (25.2 km, flat, Makuri)
27-29 March: Green to Screen (28.5 km, rolling, New York)
3-5 April: Power Punches (23.7 km, repeated small climbs, Watopia)
10-12 April: Bon Voyage (31.4 km, flat to rolling, France)
17-19 April: Cobbled Crowns (24.3 km, short sharp hills, Richmond)
24-26 April:Itza Climb Finish (30 km, medium hilly finish, watopia)
Race rules:
1. No TT Bikes.
2. Steering is enabled.
3. Smarttrainer or powermeter required.
4. Heart rate monitor required.
5. No indoor bikes without controlled resistance (cadence=power).
6. Participants must be registered on zwiftpower.com to be included in the results.
7. For podium finishers in all age groups: Elite level performances above the limits stated below
must be supported by dual power recordings uploaded to Zwiftpower
○ Men: above 5w/kg or 375w for 20min / 6w/kg or 475w for 5min / 9w/kg for 1min.
○ Women: above 4.5w/kg or 280w for 20min / 5.5w/kg or 350w for 5min / 7.5w/kg for
1min.

8. For podium finishers in all age groups: Zwift activities must be saved PUBLIC for verification.

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Redrock Loop
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off at Redrock Loop on the brand-new Gravel Mountain map. A fast, punchy gravel test to set the tone. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Redrock Loop Reverse
Date: April 27 – May 3

Back for more on the brand new Gravel Mountain map. One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/pasracing2026

name: Run In The Park
description:
Come and join the original Zwift group run! 

This event is a 5km mass start event and runs at multiple times every Saturday.  

It is open to runners of all abilities so whether you are a recreational runner or an elite athlete you are welcome on the start line.  

Get to the start gates early to chat with the other runners, you may find someone of a similar ability to group up with for the run.  

There is no time limit for completing the event so run at your own pace and enjoy the course.
