name: Zwift Beginners Ride with Project Sloth
description:
Welcome to our Project Sloth group ride.

This ride will be banded to allow people of all abilities to join.

What is banded? Banded allow us to create group rides where we all stay together as a group no matter what power output you're doing. the only requirement is that you don't stop peddling.

Project SLOTH aims to create rides that are beginner friendly, good for recovery riders and chunky people like me.

If you have any questions during the ride, please feel free to ask and we will do our best to answer them.

eventSubgroups.name: Zwift Beginners Ride with Project Sloth

name: Ascend Together - A Zwift Climbing Adventure
description:
Join forces with Ascenders and Mountain Massif Club for a weekly tradition: Ascend Together. Each week, we’ll tackle a fresh climbing challenge on Zwift, rotating routes to keep the adventure alive and spirits high. Some weeks, we'll even target special Route Badges — perfect for those climbs that seem a little too daunting when tackled solo.

This ride is open to everyone in the Zwift community because, frankly, we love your company. Expect a steady pace around 2.5 w/kg, designed to keep the group together and the energy strong. Fellow Zwift Clubs are warmly invited to co-lead and/or ride alongside us — after all, this is a celebration of everything we love about Zwift: the rides, the climbs, the camaraderie, the positivity and the community.

No matter your club, team, or solo status, we welcome you to make Ascend Together part of your weekly schedule. Let’s create a ride to remember — every single week. See you on the climb!

eventSubgroups.name: Ascend Together - A Zwift Climbing Adventure (E)

name: RO4H Sunday Social Spin
description:
Welcome to the RO4H Sunday Social Spin!
The RO4H Sunday Social Spin is a friendly, supportive group ride hosted by the Ride On 4 Health community. We aim to create a casual, respectful atmosphere that welcomes everyone—from seasoned Zwifters to first‑time group ride participants.
Pace & Expectations
Our overall average pace is typically 18–20 MPH (28–32 KPH). Terrain will naturally influence speed:
- Flats may run slightly faster within the peloton
- Climbs will remain sub‑2 W/kg
- Routes are usually flat, and we strive to be a no‑drop ride
This pace is recovery for some and a solid effort for others, so riders should be prepared to meet the average and stay with the leader’s peloton to enjoy the draft. This is a social ride, not a race—we don’t chase flyers, and flyers are free to fly.
Sweep Support
Sweepers are on hand to help anyone who drifts off the back. Their goal is to ensure no one rides alone unless they prefer to. They may help riders bridge back to the peloton or form a second pace group for a more comfortable experience.
Join the Fun
Come along for the RO4H Sunday Social Spin—you’ll likely break a sweat, hear a few dad jokes, and definitely have some fun. Welcome to the RO4H family!

eventSubgroups.name: RO4H Sunday Social Spin (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
Welcome to the RO4H Sunday Social Spin. 

The RO4H Sunday Social Spin is a social group ride hosted by the Ride On 4 Health community where a friendly, casual, supportive, and respectful atmosphere embraces everyone from the most seasoned Zwifter to the first-time group ride participant.   

The pace varies depending on terrain but will have an overall average pace of about 18-20 MPH/28-32 KPH for the ride. The pace on the flats sections will be slightly faster in the peloton and will remain sub 2 W/KG on climbs. We typically ride flat routes and try to be a no-drop ride but ultimately this pace is a recovery pace for some and a great effort for others. You should be prepared to meet the average pace and strive to stay in the ride leaders peloton to benefit from the group draft effect.  Being a social ride, we don’t chase flyers — flyers are free to fly — and this ride is certainly not a race (#zwiftisalwaysarace). 

Sweepers are available to assist those off the back. We do not want anyone to ride alone unless they choose. The sweepers will variably help riders bridge to the peloton or form a second pace group so riders do not ride alone.  

So come along for the RO4H Social Spin — you'll almost certainly break a sweat, undoubtedly hear a few dad jokes, and might even have some fun.

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Bear Mountaineers do Climb!
description:
These events will test your climbing legs for sure. One of the main reasons the Bear Mountaineers was created to get ready to climb Bear Mountain in Harriman St. Park, New York. This will prepare you to tackle your favorite climbs once you get back outside again.
Just to work into it slowly we have 4 categories separated by distance not power. We also have several ride leaders who ride at different paces and will rotate leading the A Category distance in our events.

The pace of the lead group (Category A) will be determined by the leader for that week. If you can't stay with the lead group, please join others riding at a more preferred pace. If you keep doing these events the climbing will get easier (Well that's the plan).
Andre Corba 'C+ to B' Pacer, this is the leader you will see most of the time.
Bryant Stafford 'C+' Pacer, Andre's backup but will be on every event regardless. Any issue please make him aware of them.
Mily Noyola D Pacer for C of D group

During the months from April to the end of October there may be rides without an assigned ride leader due to the outdoor riding season.

eventSubgroups.description:

We also have several leaders that will rotate thru our events. Depending on which leader you get will depend on the pace of the lead group. If you can't stay with the lead group please find others doing a pace you can handle. If you keep doing these events the climbing will get easier (Well that's the plan).

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Redrock Loop
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off at Redrock Loop on the brand-new Gravel Mountain map. A fast, punchy gravel test to set the tone. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Redrock Loop Reverse
Date: April 27 – May 3

Back for more on the brand new Gravel Mountain map. One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: BMTR Longish Adventure
description:
We invite you to join us for a fun and supportive group ride! Everyone is welcome, whether for a short time or for the entire duration.

Our experienced and friendly Ride Leaders are the foundation of our group rides. They are dedicated to making your ride enjoyable, guiding you along the way, and ensuring a fun group dynamic filled with engaging conversations and laughter. Their commitment will make your cycling experience truly memorable.

This event is structured as a longer endurance ride lasting between 2.5 and 2.75 hours. We carefully calculate the average watts per kilogram (w/kg) pace for each group, keeping most C category riders within their "Zone 2 Endurance / Zone 3 Tempo" ranges based on their Functional Threshold Power (FTP) or Zwift's calculations/assignments.

We look forward to welcoming you and having you be part of the BMTR Zwift Community!

**Join Us On Any or All of the Following Platforms!**
- Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/bmtrzwiftclub
- Strava: https://www.strava.com/clubs/BMTRZwiftClub
- Zwift: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/BMTR/home
- Instagram: https://instagram.com/bmtrclub

**To Find All Weekly BMTR Events**
- https://bmtr.events

https://www.facebook.com/WPJRCyclingTeam/

• ZwiftPower group

eventSubgroups.name: WPJR | VOLCANO ROAD TO THE HELL (E)

name: (Rubber-Banded) Ride with James' Friends!
description:
Join (some of) James' friends as we take on a number of different, varied routes across a number of different worlds.

This ride is banded. All riders will stay together regardless of their pace.

To keep up with latest events and news, please join the club here: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/389fbdf0-9cd7-4b42-8f76-4d1b6af240ae

name: Catrina's Ladies Social Ride
description:
I would like to invite ALL women to join this Social Ride & Social exchange! Our goals during the ride are as follows:

1. Recovery from Training
2. Socialization with other women riders (via Catrina’s Social Ride channel or Group Texting in Zwift)
3. Support, Development, & Growth toward becoming better cyclists/athletes in a nurturing group setting
4. Teaching skills in the Zwift program course (Riding in Groups, Draft Effect, Racing Technique, Rolling Cadence, etc.)
5. To have FUN!

This is a Social Ride, with a goal of keeping everyone together, in order to build on group riding skills. This ride is not a "race", although we may practice racing techniques. At all times, we will try to have 1-2 Ladies on the ride to assist the women on the course. Some weeks will be in workout mode but always at z2 efforts. If you yearn for opportunities to meet & socialize with other women cyclists who are passionate about riding, we strongly encourage you to join us!

Ultimately our goal is to enhance your riding experience, while having FUN!

name: Weekend Long Run
description:
Join your fellow Zwift Runners for a long tempo run designed to prepare you for those early season half-marathons. Pick a pace group and let them push you or play the role of pacer to help out everyone else.

Everyone is welcome. Warm-up on your own and finish with tempo here or use this to set yourself up for a longer run after with some other runners.

B group - 10mi/16.1km
C group - 9mi/14.5km
D group - 7mi/11.3km

eventSubgroups.name: Weekend Long Run (B)
eventSubgroups.description: #pbt

eventSubgroups.name: Weekend Long Run (C)
eventSubgroups.description: #pbt

eventSubgroups.name: Weekend Long Run (D)
eventSubgroups.description: #pbt

name: NonSequitur Social Splash
description:
Welcome to the NonSequitur Social Splash! A banded ride for the absolute beginner,
the recovering injured or any Zwifter looking for a relaxing beginner ride that hits the
sweet spot of off beat & entertaining then this is the ride for you. What will you find on
this ride you ask...bad jokes, typos, riding tips, encouragement but most importantly a
place to build your abilities and confidence.

name: WOW - High Speed Ride on TT bikes with draft
description:
The WOW Zwift events are the fastest way to collect happy miles while meeting exceptional riders/ friends on ZWIFT. While you will be assigned the fastest TT bike (Cadex) and the latest DT Swiss Disc wheel set in draft mode, you will still be upgrading your bike of choice selected in your garage before starting the ride.

Our Ds (Development of ‘Drecovery’ 😊) leave the pen first and hold the group together within D power limits. Several minutes later C’s (Crusaders) will start and keep riding as one unit guided by the power range. Further several minutes later the B train (Bombers) starts unleashing their watts. Several minutes later the A magician group (Aliens) take off and rapidly make up the delays from the groups starting ahead. When will the groups catch each other? Every catch will be an opportunity to celebrate the ONE WOW FAMILY! Can the As complete the last full segment under 8 minutes? Can the Bs go under 9 minutes? Would the time of the Cs start with a ‘9’? D group under 11 minutes?

All groups will see each other, transfers between the groups on the go will be possible. The ride leaders will help make those transitions smooth.

WOW is a no drama, full fun ride!

Double draft is on and the Cadex TT bikes will be drafting!

eventSubgroups.name: WOW - High Speed Ride on TT bikes with draft (A)
WOW - High Speed Ride on TT bikes with draft (B)
WOW - High Speed Ride on TT bikes with draft (C)
WOW - High Speed Ride on TT bikes with draft (D)

name: The Zwifty Fifty with DZR
description:
**The Zwifty Fifty: When 25 is Too Little and 100 is Too Much**

DZR brings to you the Goldilocks of Zwift racing intensity and endurance balance. With weekly races at about 50 km, the races are not too short and intensive to build stamina, and not too long and time-consuming to actually get done. They are just right. And they are on every Sunday at 14:45 CET | 8:45 AM EST.

To add to the fun and keep a high intensity throughout the race, we have included bonus second sprints. These sprints are on flats when the finish is on a KOM and on KOMs when the finish is flat.

No powerups, no zpower, no TT bikes, HRM enforced.

For all the details, including stage information, bonus sprints, rules, and registration links, visit the official website for The Zwifty Fifty at http://dzrracingseries.com/the-zwifty-fifty/

name: RollCo: Sunday Rollers Banded Badge Hunt
description:
🥽 ROLL EASY - BANDED RIDES
Welcome to The Rolling Collective (RollCo).
Our rides are built around shared momentum and riding Forward, Together.
A friendly, no-drop ride is designed to be welcoming and social. This is a great entry point if you’re new to RollCo.
What to expect:
• Banded ride - ride at your own pace
• No drop
• Leaders will communicate throughout the ride
Ride within yourself, look out for others, and enjoy the roll.

Why Join Rolling Collective?
We’re more than a club — we’re a global community of riders who support each other, grow together, and celebrate every ride, whether it’s a grind, a badge hunt, or a race. Join us on Discord, connect with fellow riders, and be part of something bigger.

Forward, Together

eventSubgroups.name: RollCo: Sunday Rollers Banded Badge Hunt (E)

name: Pride Run
description:
Join with members of the LGBTQ+ community as we stride with Pride! 

Runs are hosted by LGBTQ Zwifters Club and we invite you to join our weekly rainbow run! In some runs you will find a leader to make it social and help keep you motivated. If you are in training for a duathlon or triathlon, you can even join a Pride Ride before your run, since runs are scheduled 30 min after each Pride ride ends. Our runs are inclusive, we value diversity and courage, and we are always respectful. Social chat may involve LGBTQ+ topics so please come with an open heart and open mind. 

If you want to learn more about our Club and socials, please visit: https://lgbtq-zwifters.com/

name: Bikealicious Easy Like Sunday Morning
description:
Sunday Brunch warm up ride. Before(or after) the Mimosas and Waffles join the Bikealicious crew on some of Zwifts more “challenging” courses. An easier way to earn some of the badges that you may have put off, or said you would never return to. As a group we can help each other along the way with lively conversation and encouragement.

We will try to keep the groups pace around 2.0-2.5 w/kg, also we ride 20 or more miles depends on the route. The ride is banded to keep everyone together and the stronger riders can help pull us along!

eventSubgroups.name: Bikealicious Easy Like Sunday Morning (E)

name: ZLDR Casual Sunday
description:
Come join us for a run at your own pace (AYOP) event. Can’t go the full distance then come for any distance you choose. This is a flexible run-it-your-way all the way! We love the extra company so join this event, get on your treadmill and get ready to have some fun!

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook group:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Instagram:
https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Strava:
https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Website:
https://www.zldr.club/
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn

name: ZSUN Sunday Social Run
description: Welcome to the ZSun Sunday Social Run. There is no set pace for this run, it is just time for the community to come together and run. Join the run to unlock the ZSun Run Shirt.

name: Giant Camden Tron Hunters – get closer to the Tron
description:
The ultimate Zwift reward, the Zwift Concept Bike (aka Tron) takes a lot of effort to acquire; you have to climb 50,000mts of elevation to get your hands on this prize. Team Giant Camden organizes weekly rides around the hilliest routes to rack up elevation and give you a good endurance ride, so this is the perfect opportunity if you’re hunting for the Tron or some of the toughest route badges on Zwift and want some company.

**This is not a race** but some riders will go full steam, the majority of us will be taking a steady ride and chat in game. Find riders around your ability and share the work.

**Don’t forget to have the Everest Challenge selected before joining: https://zwiftinsider.com/tron-bike/ **

Join team Giant Camden on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/1208978142879716/

name: THE COALITION Mega Pretzel Badge Hunt
description:
Welcome to THE COALITION’s Mega Pretzel badge hunt!

Join us as we tackle one of Watopia’s longer routes where we will explore the jungle, climb the Epic KOM from both directions and in the middle enjoy the Volcano KOM. 

Yellow beacon is aiming to AVERAGE 2.3-2.6wkg (at 85kg) at the end of the ride. The climbs will be at the top end or slightly higher than that pace. We hope to keep as many riders together as possible but if you are riding above or below this pace we would encourage you to find your own groups to enjoy the ride with. We will attempt to regroup after climbs should things really break apart.

THE COALITION is one of the fastest-growing teams on Zwift, with over 40 teams in the Zwift Racing League and are represented in all other major racing series. Our DNA is in racing but we also foster a fun and inclusive environment for all. If you would like to join us, then find out more at https://coalitionracing.com/

RR!

#coalition

eventSubgroups.name: THE COALITION Mega Pretzel Badge Hunt

name: Level Racing Chase Race
description:
Chase race with riders starting in 4 pens based on CE categories A, B, C, D.  Handicap times are applied between pens.  D cat riders leave first, followed by C, then B and finally A.  The object for each group is to work together to chase and catch the groups ahead of you.  The D cat group will try to stay in front of the other groups.  If the handicaps are correct, then all teams will come together somewhere in the final kilometer,  As this happens, the race changes from working in groups to an individual race, where the first across the line is the winner

To help you chat and work together with others in your pen, Level offer a Discord voice service.  You are welcome to use this if you want.  It’s equally ok to race without it and listen to your music or whatever else you do when racing.  For those who want to use Discord voice, you can join the Level Chase Race Discord server here.  Make sure you join the correct voice channel for your pen!

https://discord.gg/5XRFshA4

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Banded Bike MS Badge Hunt
description:
Multiple Sclerosis does not stop, so neither will we as we ride together for this great cause. Bike MS is excited to offer another series of rides to keep cyclists connected year round with hopes of also connecting at one of the many Bike MS Events across the US in person. The Rubberband effect will be on during the ride to help keep cyclists of all abilities together as we explore some of the longer and more difficult routes throughout the many worlds of Zwift. 
Whatever pace you ride we welcome you to come join a group of passionate cyclists looking to raise funds and awareness for those living with multiple sclerosis. We hope you enjoy the ride and if so, please join Bike MS Zwift Club for year round training and community engagement. Come for the miles of smiles and stay for the XP. 
Bike MS is the largest charity cycling series across the United States consisting of 50 rides and raising over $50 million in 2023 for the National MS Society. Bike MS supports the National MS Society's mission to Cure MS while empowering all people affected by MS to live their best lives. Whether you want to cross the Finish Line in person or take on a new challenge from anywhere — there’s a Bike MS event that’s right for you! 
Check out www.bikeMS.org and experience the ride of your life. Use Zwift25 for a discount on your Bike MS Ride entry fee.
Come join us for weekly Club Rides: Bike MS ZC
#dontjustride #bikeMS #whateverittakes #strongertogether

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Redrock Loop
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off at Redrock Loop on the brand-new Gravel Mountain map. A fast, punchy gravel test to set the tone. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Redrock Loop Reverse
Date: April 27 – May 3

Back for more on the brand new Gravel Mountain map. One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: KISS Racing 100
description:
Keep it Super Simple. Race hard and race fair.
Please use ZwiftPower's Minimum Category on your profile for race entry.
Cats A, and B will require a power source (power meter or smart trainer) and heart rate monitor.
Cat C will require power source (power meter or smart trainer) and heart rate monitor for podium spots.
Podium finishers in Cat A are expected to dual (power) record and to make analysis publicly available on ZwiftPower with power sources correctly labelled; they must also be using a direct drive trainer or power meter.
**Elite level performances are also expected to be supported by dual (power) recordings from reputable power sources or easily verifiable outside data. This applies to those with a history of such performances.
** 5.2 w/kg (20 mins); 6 w/kg (5 mins); 9.8 w/kg (1 min)
Zwift Activities must be saved publicly.
Please also maintain an open and active Strava profile to facilitate organiser inquiry.

name: Phoenix Rising Tronathon (Banded)
description:
Tronathon is a social, supportive banded ride. We help riders build strength, endurance and comfort in riding hills. We also mix in a healthy amount of entertainment and distraction. Tronathon was established in 2020 to help riders earn their Tron bike while also earning the Avid or Masochist badges. This continues to be our goal. All are welcome whether you are a newer cyclist, question if you can reach the summit of one of Zwift's hills, currently hate climbing, or just want to support other riders. Everyone has a story about their first Alpe, Tron quest or the climbing gains they've made and we want to hear yours. We are a fun loving community and welcome all who might want to join us!

For more information on all our weekly events, join our club Phoenix Rising on Zwift Companion.

Join us on Discord during the ride: https://discord.gg/mNMva8ekJR
Check us out on Facebook https://www.facebook.com/groups/615972979730125/

name: ZZRC Sunday Cruise Control
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Cruise Control 2-2.5. This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 2-2.5 on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints , but the average for the whole ride will be as advertised.

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced .

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch .

This is where our awesome ZZRC sweeps and Ride Buddies will help you return to group using their draft. They will give you clear encouraging instructions to aid this process .

Our crew are a caring bunch who dedicated a lot of hours to this club , but want you to have the best ride experience on Zwift

You will be fully supported and encouraged throughout the ride by the Leader, Sweeps and Ride Buddies .

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you .

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group .

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON .

We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord channel
https://discord.gg/QdeS3PtXTX

TOP TIPS
1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!
2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone
3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app.

name: BMTR Short Adventure
description:
The BMTR Short Adventure focuses on enjoying a fun group experience while cycling at an endurance pace.

Our ride leaders are experienced, friendly, and ready to ensure you have a great time. They'll help keep the ride fun and at the right pace for your group!

This event is mainly about "Zone 2 Endurance" riding, which means we’ll be cycling at a comfortable pace. Each category has an average watts per kilogram (w/kg) range designed to keep most riders in their "Zone 2 Endurance" or low "Zone 3 Tempo" zones. Just choose the category that fits your Functional Threshold Power (FTP) or the one that Zwift assigns to you, and you’ll be all set for an enjoyable ride!


**Join Us on Any or All of the Following Platforms!**
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/bmtrzwiftclub
Strava: https://www.strava.com/clubs/BMTRZwiftClub
Zwift: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/BMTR/home
Instagram: https://instagram.com/bmtrclub

**To Find All Weekly BMTR Events**
https://bmtr.events

eventSubgroups.name: BMTR Short Adventure (D)

Our very experienced, friendly and engaging ride leaders make sure a good time is had by all and keep the ride within the advertised average pace!

name: Endure ES Fondo
description:
This weekly group ride is your opportunity to tackle that Century distance of 100 miles or 160.9 km, while having company along the way. Whether you are a seasoned cyclist or looking to complete your first ever Century, this group ride has something to offer to Zwifters of all experience and abilities. There is no designated pace for this ride, so we encourage you to ride at your own pace, and enjoy the ride. 

Double draft is on in this event to encourage everyone to work together. 

If you enjoy the ride and want easy access to future Endure ES Fondos, make sure to join our club by clicking on this link: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/3510fe3d-531b-4a4a-8505-64d200733aec/home 

Ride on!

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Neon After Party
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Turbocharged Sunday Social
description:
Join several of the Turbo riders for this Turbocharged Sunday Social group ride aka TSS, because who doesn’t love a little TSS!?  

Come for the banter, stay for the after party.  Each week will be a 2.5 hour rubber band group ride, meaning you can go whatever pace you’d like and we all stay together.  Each week we will be on a route in Watopia and after the event ends, anyone that wants to go party pace, we will be headed to Epic Forward KOM for a spirited pace to the top!

name: The WATT Squad Sunday Smash
description:
Finish your week with strong watts on pure races of approximately 1h30.

Join other riders for challenging weekly races on various routes.
Sprinters, all-rounders, climbers you will need to unleash your watts on the perfect spot to go for victory.

To appear in the official results on ZwiftPower, it is mandatory to:

Be registered on ZwiftPower.
Use a heart rate monitor (HRM).
Connect a smart trainer or power meter as a power source. (Dual recording is encouraged).

Make sure you meet all requirements to avoid disqualification.

Join The Watt Squad in your Companion App and look for our other events as well. Interested in joining, then reach out to us on https://www.facebook.com/groups/thewattsquad

name: ZSUN Sunday Sundae
description:
The Sunday Sundae hits your sweetspot with a big, friendly, supportive and hilly group ride! We cruise the flats in a big blob at steady base pace (circa 2.3-2.5 w/kg); we hit long climbs at sweetspot to threshold interval pace; and we regroup after descents (see below). The climbing pace will be clearly announced before and during each climb and will *usually* be in the 2.8-3.2 w/kg. Course varies from week to week, with total duration normally in the 90-120 minutes range, except for special events.

Please follow the ride leader's instructions and remember that this is not a race. The goal is to ride together, support each other and have fun! Remember also that power figures in this description are those of the beacon and those numbers will vary based on, amongst other factors, rider weight.

REGROUPING
Things will stretch out on the climbs, and that's normal. After the descents, those in front of the beacon will be expected to wait, and there will also be a period of "slow roll" (2.0 w/kg) to allow those not too far behind the beacon to rejoin the peleton for the next flat.

Note that there is currently no sweep for this ride. We aim to stay together, but if you get gapped by the blob, losing the draft effect of the group will make it very difficult to get back and you may be dropped. If/when that happens, you should try to form a gruppetto with others holding a similar pace in order to complete the ride.

name: Group Run 5k
description:
DESCRIPTION

Join us for a 5k group run.   It's run-at-your-own-pace and all are welcome! 

Pick the closest pace group for you, but all groups will start together and you are free to fall back and run ahead. The pace groups designate average pace for the duration of the run. Most groups will start slower and finish faster than the indicated pace. Feel free to mix in some intervals and race your friends.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Times Square Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: 3R Endurance Steady Ride
description:
3R Chase Endurance Ride

Join us for an exhilarating 3R Group Ride Event, where teamwork isn’t just encouraged, it’s essential!

This description does not include the C endurance ride, this is as usual a normal C endurance ride.

In this Chase Endurance Ride, we embark on a 100km journey, following the same course as our Steady State B group. The aim? To gradually close the gap and merge with the Steady State B Group around the 80km mark, setting the stage for an exciting final stretch.

This ride is about group effort and shared responsibility. We expect each rider to contribute, taking turns on the front with pulls of approximately 4.5-5.0 w/kg. Remember, it’s a chase, not a free ride, so we kindly ask that you join in the spirit of collective effort rather than just drafting throughout.

When our group catches up with the Steady State B group, it’s a chance to mix things up! If you’re feeling strong, keep up the pace with the A group. Alternatively, if you’re in the mood for a more relaxed ride, feel free to settle in with the B group.

Remember, this isn’t a race, but a part of our #ProStyleTraining series, designed to support your training goals in a fun, social setting. We love a bit of friendly banter and encourage you to share your experiences and feedback on our Facebook page at https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/. Let’s ride, raise, race and enjoy together with #Team3R.

eventSubgroups.name: 3R Endurance Steady Ride (B)
eventSubgroups.description: This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise. #3r

name: The Stampede Challenge by Herd Racing
description:
Join Herd Racing for our monthly TT challenge. For the month of January
it's 2 laps of Handful of Gravel. You can race this as many times as you'd
like and try to beat your time and your best time counts in the monthly
standings https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=3093

For these events drafting is turned off, so it's just you and the clock. Is
the gravel, the MTB or the TT bike fastest? Maybe even a road bike?!?!
Pick your fastest equipment and give it your best shot! Join us in the Herd
Racing League facebook or Discord server to chat pacing strategies, gear,
and more.

name: JUST DO iTT
description: JUST DO iTT is a weekly individual time trial forming a mini-league of 4-5 stages each month. All racers times for the 4-5 stages will be combined to crown a monthly champion. The races will be a combination of bunch starts with no draft and staggered starts using Zwift's time trial module. The routes will vary from week to week in distance and elevation.

name: The Mountain Massif
description:
The biggest spectacles in bike racing involve mountains. This race is designed to create moments to remember.

This race will see you tackle longer routes, with the odd mountain or hill thrown in.

This event is designed to test your racing tactics and stamina. Be sure to save enough energy so you can go "full throttle" when required, battling fellow racers for victory. This is the chance to push yourself beyond your perceived limits and reach new heights.

Rules: 
Heart Rate monitor required and Zwift Power for official results, winner of each category. 


More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Instagram, Mountain_Massif and join our Zwift Club, by clicking the link below
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Level Racing Women Only Chase Race
description:
Chase race with riders starting in 4 pens based on CE categories A, B, C, D.  Handicap times are applied between pens.  D cat riders leave first, followed by C, then B and finally A.  The object for each group is to work together to chase and catch the groups ahead of you.  The D cat group will try to stay in front of the other groups.  If the handicaps are correct, then all teams will come together somewhere in the final kilometer,  As this happens, the race changes from working in groups to an individual race, where the first across the line is the winner

To help you chat and work together with others in your pen, Level offer a Discord voice service.  You are welcome to use this if you want.  It’s equally ok to race without it and listen to your music or whatever else you do when racing.  For those who want to use Discord voice, you can join the Level Chase Race Discord server here.  Make sure you join the correct voice channel for your pen!

https://discord.gg/5XRFshA4

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Redrock Loop
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off at Redrock Loop on the brand-new Gravel Mountain map. A fast, punchy gravel test to set the tone. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Redrock Loop Reverse
Date: April 27 – May 3

Back for more on the brand new Gravel Mountain map. One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: 3R Racing
description:
This RACE is brought to you by 3R to help draw attention to our charity partners. One example is how Buffalo Bikes (provided by the charity WBR for £100/each) can benefit the world's transport needy families.
Expect this RACE to be highly competitive across all CATs. In order to make the racing as fair as possible, racers on zPower, lacking heart rate monitors, riding an an inappropriate category, or riding very unrealistically may be disqualified.
Final results will be available on zwiftpower.com. We hope that everyone gets a great workout and has some good fun in this part of 3R’s #prostyletraining offering. #rideon #RideRaceRaise

name: RO4H Rookie Group Ride
description: Welcome to the RO4H Rookie Group Ride! This event is a relaxed, supportive teaching ride designed to help Zwifters of all experience levels learn the basics of Zwifting and group riding in a friendly, judgment-free environment. We aim for an average pace of 16–19 mph (26–30 kph) in the leader's peloton. Routes are mostly flat, though rolling terrain may cause natural pace changes. Sweepers are available to help riders reconnect or ride comfortably at their own pace. If you're new to group rides or unsure about cycling terms, don't worry—you're in the right place. Many riders here are beginners, and no one is judging. Just click the orange square with the white plus sign and join us. Rookie Tip: We recommend using the Zwift Companion App for communication, navigation, and situational awareness. You can find it at: https://www.zwift.com/companion. Join the RO4H Rookie Ride, learn, ride, and have fun!

name: PACK🐺 Banded Buddies
description:
Join the PACK for some fun weekend adventures on a wide variety of routes as we go badge hunting! This event is banded so everyone at any fitness level is welcome. As long as you turn those pedals you will stay with the group and even if you have a dropout, the sweep team will do its best to help you back to the group. You can sign up early via the web or mobile app and late join is enabled.

All riders will appear in the same kit (unlocked at the finish) and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please visit the Coffee Shop if you need a break especially on longer climbs. #coffeetogether 😊

Finally, if you are just starting your Zwift experience, please join our Facebook group and PACK Club for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

PACK Club:
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/Pack/join

Facebook Group #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

PACK Events:
https://zwift.com/events/tag/pack

Discord Voice Chat:
https://discord.gg/cce7ers

ZwiftPower Team:
https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=2928

name: LGBTQz Ride with Pride
description:
Everyone is welcome to join LGBTQ Zwifters and friends as we Ride with Pride all
year round in our Sunday social ride!
Our yellow beacons aim to maintain an average social pace of around 1.6 - 1.8
W/Kg, and we encourage everyone to ride close to yellow and enjoy being part of
our rainbow blob. Please be aware that we frequently use the rubberband in our group rides. You can check the event details to see if this is the case for this ride by using the link 'Toggle Event Details' at the end of the event description on zwift.com. Ride leaders may have quizzes or other fun
stuff planned for you, so please do get involved using the companion app chat
function. Our rides are inclusive, we value diversity and courage, and we are always
respectful. Ride chat may involve LGBTQIA+ topics so please come with an open
heart and open mind.
If you want to learn more about what to expect on one of our rides, or find links to our
Club and socials, please visit: https://lgbtq-zwifters.com/

name: Team DRAFT Sunday Race
description:
Welcome to the Sunday Race brought to you by Team DRAFT!
We encourage that all participants head over to zwiftpower.com and register. For more information see: https://zwiftinsider.com/zwiftpower-signup/ Riders who have not completed the Zwiftpower registration will not be included in the official race results. We also ask that your Zwift activities be saved publicly, and that you open and maintain a Strava account to allow for organizer analysis. Any rider exhibiting questionable performance during a the race may be disqualified.

RULES:
- No zPower riders.
- Heart rate monitors mandatory for all categories
- 5min and 20min CEVAZ limits apply. Riding in the wrong category will lead to disqualification.

RACE CATEGORIES:
A: 4.0 W/kg to 5.0 W/kg
B: 3.2 W/kg to 3.99 W/kg
C: 2.5 W/kg to 3.19 W/kg
D: Under 2.49 W/kg
